description: Guilds, orders, societies, and structured membership groups.
factions: Commune
Members pooling labor and land equally, thriving on solidarity but cracking under selfish ambition.
Guild
Craftsmen or merchants enforcing standards and controlling trade, quick to punish outsiders who undercut their prices.
Order
Sworn members following a strict hierarchy and code, serving a sacred or martial cause unto death.
Brotherhood
Oath-bound brothers guarding each other's secrets and interests, trusting no one beyond the fraternity.
Sisterhood
Women bound by vow and purpose, protecting their own with fierce solidarity and shared resolve.
Circle
Practitioners gathered around a shared discipline, trading knowledge behind doors closed to outsiders.
Lodge
A local chapter meeting in secret and following rituals handed down from a distant founding body.
Chapter
A regional branch carrying out the parent body's will while adapting orders to local conditions.
Sect
Believers who have split from the mainstream, following doctrines the wider faith considers dangerous or heretical.
Society
Members united under a shared mission, building influence through collective reputation and internal loyalty.
Association
Professionals banded together to set standards and protect their common interests against outside pressure.
Troupe
Performers traveling and rehearsing as one tight-knit company, living or dying by the crowd's applause.
Commission
Appointed agents carrying out a specific mandate with narrow but potent authority.
Committee
Delegates wrangling over a focused charge, where progress stalls until enough factions agree.
Board
A ruling body steering an institution's course, each seat representing a different sphere of influence.
Panel
A small group of judges or experts weighing evidence and rendering binding decisions.
Caucus
Faction members meeting behind closed doors to align their votes before the public session.
Symposium
Scholars and practitioners convening to debate ideas and forge alliances over shared intellectual passion.
Conclave
Powerful figures gathering in secret to make decisions that reshape the world outside.
Forum
An open assembly where any voice may speak, though the loudest and boldest tend to dominate.
Institute
Researchers and advocates advancing a cause through study, pushing the boundaries of accepted knowledge.
Cooperative
Farmers or artisans sharing tools, labor, and risk while splitting profits and hardships equally among members.
Collective
Skilled craftspeople pooling their talents on joint projects, blending techniques no single artisan could master alone.
Circus
Acrobats, beasts, and spectacle rolling from town to town, dazzling crowds while hiding darker dealings backstage.
Social Club
Elites mingling behind gilded doors, trading gossip and favors over wine and false smiles.
Academic Circle
Scholars collaborating on specialized research and hoarding breakthroughs until ready to claim the glory.
Philosophical School
Thinkers championing a worldview through argument and mentorship, clashing bitterly with rival schools of thought.
Conservation Society
Devoted stewards protecting endangered lands, ruins, or traditions against the tide of progress and greed.
Innovation Collective
Inventors and visionaries racing to push boundaries, often breaking old rules faster than new ones form.
Literary Society
Writers and bibliophiles championing the written word, wielding stories and verse as tools of influence.
Denomination
A recognized branch of a larger faith, following its own rites while sharing a common creed.
Fraternity
Initiates bonded through shared trials and secret rites, forging loyalties that outlast any single cause.
Sorority
Sisters pledged to one another through ritual and tradition, building networks reaching far beyond their halls.
Foundation
Wealthy patrons funding a cause, wielding charity as both altruism and quiet political leverage.
Choir
Voices joined in rehearsed harmony for sacred or civic rites, their songs carrying weight beyond mere melody.
Congregation
The faithful gathering regularly for worship, forming the backbone of a temple's spiritual and social power.
Flock
Devotees following a spiritual shepherd whose guidance shapes their daily lives and deepest loyalties.
Fellowship
Companions bound by shared conviction and supporting one another, blending friendship with mission.
Club
Members gathered around a common passion, enforcing unspoken rules about who belongs and who does not.
Ensemble
Musicians, actors, or dancers performing as one coordinated body, each member essential to the whole.
Orchestra
Dozens of musicians playing under a conductor's command, blending individual talent into overwhelming collective sound.
Chorus
Singers or dancers moving in unison, their synchronized performance commanding attention and stirring emotion.
Organization
A structured body pursuing a defined purpose, its hierarchy channeling individual effort into collective will.
Institution
An enduring body shaping society through tradition and authority, outlasting the individuals who serve it.
Community
Neighbors sharing a common identity and looking out for their own, closing ranks against outside threats.
Alchemists' Circle
Secretive practitioners experimenting with volatile substances and forbidden transmutations, guarding formulas with paranoid zeal.
Branch
A satellite arm extending a larger body's reach into new territory, adapting the mission to local ground.
